summary: Blow Away and China>Rider shine; solid spring ’89 set with high energy
blurb: The first set rolls from a rollicking Let The Good Times Roll into a scorching Blow Away, then moves through Loser to a tight Cassidy finish. The second set delivers the classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, deep jams on Eyes Of The World and Dear Mr. Fantasy, and a powerful Box of Rain encore. Brent’s keyboards are on fire and Phil’s bass drives the momentum throughout.
review: April 8‑1989 at Riverfront Arena captured one of the Spring ’89 tour’s most energetic outings. Jerry Garcia’s voice showed a hint of strain typical for this era, but his lead guitar remained ferocious – especially on Blow Away where he “never misses a beat” and throws in a pedal stomp that fuels the crowd. Brent Mydland was the engine of the night, delivering an “on‑fire” B3 solo on Wang Dang Doodle and soaring vocal harmonies that lift the China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition to “unbelievable highs.” Phil Lesh’s bass shone on the Box of Rain encore, anchoring a heartfelt finale. The dual drummers kept a solid foundation, with Mickey’s assertive fills driving the Rider segment and Billy laying down a tight groove throughout both sets. While a few reviewers noted a couple of weaker moments – a muffled start to L.L. Rain (not in this set) and occasional vocal strain – the consensus is that the show’s high‑octane jams, standout songs, and overall flow make it a must‑listen for any Deadhead looking for a quintessential late‑’80s performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of seven recordings are positive, highlighting Blow Away, China>Rider, Box of Rain and strong band interplay; two mixed reviews note vocal strain but still praise overall energy. Consistent acclaim across multiple sources justifies a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear, well‑balanced soundboard tape with no dropouts; captures the band’s tight mix and highlights the standout Loser, China>Rider transition, Good Times opening, and Box of Rain encore.

key_highlights: China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue is a show‑stopper
Box of Rain encore delivers emotional climax
Eyes Of The World showcases tight group improvisation

band_performance.Jerry: Voice shows a bit of hoarseness but his lead guitar stays fierce, delivering powerful solos on Blow Away and driving the band’s momentum.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is prominent throughout, especially on the Box of Rain encore where it pushes the show over the edge, providing solid foundation for the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals are strong on crowd‑pleasers like Let The Good Times Roll, adding humor and energy to the set.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work is fire‑filled – a standout B3 solo on Wang Dang Doodle, percolating transitions in China>Rider, and soaring vocal harmonies that lift the performance.
band_performance.Mickey: Assertive drumming drives the Rider segment to ‘unbelievable highs’ and adds texture throughout the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a solid, tight groove that underpins the high‑energy jams, especially in the second set.
